# flame Phase 4-B-3-2 integration design (build pipeline mechanism)

> Design draft for the next implementation cycle. No code change.
> Builds on PHASE4B3_EMISSION_DESIGN.md (commit `828717fb`) +
> boxing-only pivot (commit `f525a656`).

## Key audit finding (2026-05-17)

hexa_v2-emitted C uses a **single-TU inline pattern**:

```c
// Generated by HEXA self-host compiler
#include "runtime.c"

HexaVal t_zeros(HexaVal n);
HexaVal t_get(HexaVal t, HexaVal i);
// ... rest of hexa-source-derived signatures and bodies
```

Every emitted .c file inlines the entire 22K-line `runtime.c` via
`#include "runtime.c"`. This means:

1. `HexaVal` struct definition + `hexa_int()` constructor are visible
   in the same translation unit as all flame fn bodies.
2. Trampoline emission can append .c text directly to the same TU
   (no separate compilation unit, no link-time symbol resolution
   issues, no header propagation work).
3. There is no .h boundary the trampoline needs to cross.

This is the cleanest possible integration path: **emit trampoline C
text and concatenate to the IPCP-rewritten .c before clang**.

## Integration mechanism

Extend `tool/flame_phase4b_build.sh` with a Step 3.5:

```
1. module_loader flatten        → /tmp/<stem>_expanded.hexa
2. flame_phase4b_ipcp rewriter   → /tmp/<stem>_ipcp.hexa
3. hexa_v2 transpile             → build/artifacts/<stem>_ipcp.c
3.5 (NEW) flame_phase4b3_emit    → build/artifacts/<stem>_trampoline.c.txt
3.6 (NEW) concat                 → build/artifacts/<stem>_ipcp_b3.c
4. clang -O2                     → output binary
```

The emit step generates a .c snippet (not .h) with extern declarations
+ trampoline bodies. Concat appends to the IPCP .c before clang.

## Trampoline body form (no-perf, fallback)

For Phase 4-B-3-2-first (boxing-only, no leaf spec), trampoline does
nothing useful — it unboxes args at entry, boxes them back, calls the
original HexaVal fn. Net: same perf as IPCP (no boxing gain yet,
since the body is still HexaVal-internal). Purpose: **verify build
integration works end-to-end**, byte-eq holds by construction.

```c
// Auto-generated by flame_phase4b3_emit (Phase 4-B-3-2-first)
// Tag: F-RFC047-EMIT-TRAMPOLINE-INTEGRATION

static inline void flame_block_T16_d32_nh4_nkv2_h64_fwd(
    int X_id, int Bp_id, int Bc_id, int cos_id, int sin_id
) {
    nn_decoder_block_fwd(
        hexa_int(X_id), hexa_int(Bp_id), hexa_int(Bc_id),
        hexa_int(cos_id), hexa_int(sin_id),
        hexa_int(16), hexa_int(32), hexa_int(4),
        hexa_int(2), hexa_int(64)
    );
}
```

This compiles cleanly because `HexaVal`, `hexa_int`, and
`nn_decoder_block_fwd` are all in scope (single-TU inline pattern).

## Caller wire-up (deferred — Phase 4-B-3-2-second)

For the trampoline to actually be called, the IPCP source's
`nn_decoder_block_fwd(Xc, Bp_l, Bc_l, u_cos, u_sin, hexa_int(16),
hexa_int(32), hexa_int(4), hexa_int(2), hexa_int(64))` call site
needs to become `flame_block_T16_d32_nh4_nkv2_h64_fwd((int)Xc.i,
(int)Bp_l.i, (int)Bc_l.i, (int)u_cos.i, (int)u_sin.i)`.

Two paths for this rewrite:

**(W1) IPCP source rewrite** — extend `flame_phase4b_ipcp.hexa` with
a Phase 2 pass that detects literal-tuple block calls and rewrites
them to specialized fn calls. Hexa-source level.

**(W2) Post-hexa_v2 .c rewrite** — text-rewrite the hexa_v2-emitted
.c to replace `nn_decoder_block_fwd(...)` literal-tuple calls with
the specialized fn name. C-source level.

W2 simpler (no hexa-lang re-parse), but more brittle (depends on
hexa_v2's call-site formatting). W1 more robust but requires the
IPCP rewriter to understand a richer syntax.

**Recommended Phase 4-B-3-2-second path: W1** — fits the existing
IPCP tool's text-substitution model. The rewrite is identical to
IPCP except it changes the call expression instead of the body.

## Phase 4-B-3-2-third: real boxing-elim body

Once trampoline integration is verified (byte-eq via fallback to
HexaVal fn), Phase 4-B-3-2-third replaces the trampoline body with
direct farr_table operations + leaf fn specializations:

```c
static inline void flame_block_T16_d32_nh4_nkv2_h64_fwd(
    int X_id, int Bp_id, int Bc_id, int cos_id, int sin_id
) {
    // Direct farr_table dereferences — no HexaVal boxing
    double* X    = farr_table_data(X_id);
    double* Bp   = farr_table_data(Bp_id);
    double* Bc   = farr_table_data(Bc_id);
    double* cos_ = farr_table_data(cos_id);
    double* sin_ = farr_table_data(sin_id);

    // call specialized leaf fns (also primitive-typed)
    flame_rmsnorm_d32_fwd(X, Bp + g1_off, Bc + rin_off, ...);
    flame_linear_d32_d32_fwd(Bc + rin_off, Bp + Wq_off, Bc + Q_off, ...);
    // ... etc., 7-12 leaf calls, all primitive-typed
}
```

Each leaf fn also emitted as `flame_<leaf>_<shape>(...)` specialization.
Captures the 16M box/unbox ops per run → expected 4× wall ceiling
per boxing-elim measurement (commit `07cdd405`).

## Falsifier matrix (Phase 4-B-3-2 sub-phases)

| sub-phase | falsifier | required |
|---|---|---|
| 4-B-3-2-first | F-RFC047-EMIT-TRAMPOLINE-COMPILES | `clang -c` succeeds on concatenated .c |
| 4-B-3-2-first | F-RFC047-EMIT-TRAMPOLINE-LINKS | full binary builds without symbol errors |
| 4-B-3-2-second | F-RFC047-CALL-REWRITE-BYTE-EQ | binary with trampoline called produces stdout byte-id to baseline |
| 4-B-3-2-third | F-RFC047-BOXING-ELIM-BYTE-EQ | binary with primitive-typed body produces byte-id (strict; or fp-tol per RFC 040) |
| 4-B-3-2-third | F-RFC047-BOXING-ELIM-WALL | 5-run avg ≥3× over Phase 4-A-bwd baseline (target 12.574s → ≤4.2s; 4× per measurement gives ≤3.14s) |

## Need-not-do this cycle

- HexaVal-internal helper inlining (`farr_get`, `farr_set` body inline) —
  these dominate inner loops, but rewriting them is runtime.c work,
  not flame work. Future RFC.
- d=768·12L config emission — d=32·3L proves the mechanism; scaling
  is Phase 4-B-3-4.
- GPU dispatch — orthogonal Phase 4-D.

## Estimated effort breakdown (revised — was 3-4 cycles total)

| sub-phase | effort | risk |
|---|---|---|
| 4-B-3-2-first  trampoline emit + concat + compile | 1 cycle | low |
| 4-B-3-2-second IPCP-source caller rewrite (W1) | 1-2 cycles | mid |
| 4-B-3-2-third  primitive-typed body + leaf specs | 2-3 cycles | mid-high |
| 4-B-3-3        bwd specialization mirror | 1-2 cycles | mid |
| **total** | **5-8 cycles** | mid |

Note: this is **more cycles than the original "3-4 cycles" estimate**
in PHASE4B3_EMISSION_DESIGN.md "Design pivot" section. The pivot
focused on dropped scope (no full inline, no stack scratch), not on
caller wire-up + leaf specialization complexity. Honest revision:
boxing-only is simpler in mechanism but the build integration +
leaf specialization is non-trivial.

Reward (per measurement-anchored compound 4.0×):
- expected wall: 12.574s baseline → ~3.14s (4× speedup)
- flame vs anima: 0.568× → 0.142× (~7× faster)
- vs RFC 047 §137 ≥3× target: 33% margin
